verses.text: God calls those who seek righteousness to 'look unto the rock whence ye are hewn,' to Abraham whom He blessed; so He will comfort Zion's waste places. He recalls His past faithfulness. The God who made a nation from one man will surely restore His people.

God calls those who seek righteousness to 'look unto the rock whence ye are hewn,' to Abraham whom He blessed; so He will comfort Zion's waste places. He recalls His past faithfulness. The God who made a nation from one man will surely restore His people.

God calls those who seek righteousness to 'look unto the rock whence ye are hewn,' to Abraham whom He blessed; so He will comfort Zion's waste places. He recalls His past faithfulness. The God who made a nation from one man will surely restore His people.

His salvation is for ever, while 'the heavens shall vanish away like smoke'; His righteousness shall not be abolished. The eternal outlasts the temporal. When earth and sky pass, God's saving righteousness still stands.

His salvation is for ever, while 'the heavens shall vanish away like smoke'; His righteousness shall not be abolished. The eternal outlasts the temporal. When earth and sky pass, God's saving righteousness still stands.

His salvation is for ever, while 'the heavens shall vanish away like smoke'; His righteousness shall not be abolished. The eternal outlasts the temporal. When earth and sky pass, God's saving righteousness still stands.

Fear not the reproach of men, for 'the moth shall eat them up like a garment,' but God's righteousness endures. Their scorn is short-lived. The opinion of frail men should not shake those who know the eternal God.

Fear not the reproach of men, for 'the moth shall eat them up like a garment,' but God's righteousness endures. Their scorn is short-lived. The opinion of frail men should not shake those who know the eternal God.

'Awake, awake... O arm of the LORD,' who cut Rahab and dried the sea; so the ransomed shall return to Zion with singing. He pleads for fresh deliverance. The God of the exodus will redeem His people again, and sorrow shall flee away.

'Awake, awake... O arm of the LORD,' who cut Rahab and dried the sea; so the ransomed shall return to Zion with singing. He pleads for fresh deliverance. The God of the exodus will redeem His people again, and sorrow shall flee away.

'Awake, awake... O arm of the LORD,' who cut Rahab and dried the sea; so the ransomed shall return to Zion with singing. He pleads for fresh deliverance. The God of the exodus will redeem His people again, and sorrow shall flee away.
'I, even I, am he that comforteth you'; why then fear a man who shall die like grass? He chides their dread. To fear mortal man while forgetting the eternal Comforter is needless folly.
'I, even I, am he that comforteth you'; why then fear a man who shall die like grass? He chides their dread. To fear mortal man while forgetting the eternal Comforter is needless folly.
'I, even I, am he that comforteth you'; why then fear a man who shall die like grass? He chides their dread. To fear mortal man while forgetting the eternal Comforter is needless folly.
The LORD who stirs the sea has put His words in His servant's mouth and covered him with His hand. His prophet is kept and equipped. The same power that rules the deep guards and uses His messenger.
The LORD who stirs the sea has put His words in His servant's mouth and covered him with His hand. His prophet is kept and equipped. The same power that rules the deep guards and uses His messenger.

Jerusalem, who drank 'the cup of his fury' to the dregs, is bidden awake; her sons lay fainting in the streets. Judgment has emptied her. Yet the call to awake hints that the night of wrath is nearly past.

Jerusalem, who drank 'the cup of his fury' to the dregs, is bidden awake; her sons lay fainting in the streets. Judgment has emptied her. Yet the call to awake hints that the night of wrath is nearly past.

God promises to take 'the cup of trembling' from her hand and give it to her tormentors who trod her down. The cup is passed to her foes. The God who chastened His people will turn the bitterness upon their oppressors.
